name: LES MILLS XR DANCE - Fitness & Rhythm

reviews.review_description: The game itself is excellent, but I have to leave a negative review because it can only be played using controllers. I my opinion there is no clear reason why hand tracking isn’t supported, and relying on controllers takes away from the immersion, especially when you’re trying to feel fully engaged while dancing to the music. I will happily update this to a 5-star review if hand tracking support is added in the future.

developer_response.body:
Hi! Thanks for your feedback. We understand your point about immersion, but as you can imagine, the use of controllers is required to ensure the experience is as precise as possible. The controllers act as reliable trackers, allowing us to accurately detect hits, timing, and movement, which is key for gameplay quality.

It’s also important to consider that not all devices have advanced enough hand-tracking technology to provide the same level of accuracy and consistency. For this type of experience, precision is critical, and controllers help guarantee that. That said, we truly appreciate your input and will keep it in mind.

reviews.review_description:
I’d leave a negative review if possible as I bought the game but am unable to launch and play it as I cannot ACCEPT THE TERMS. When I try to click to accept. Nothing. 

I’ve reached out over and over and nobody will help me. It’s useless and I’m out the money.

developer_response.body:
Hi! We’re really sorry for the inconvenience. We haven’t received any email from you so far.

Please contact us at support@odderslab.com with your username and, if possible, a video showing the issue. That will help us investigate it properly and assist you as soon as possible so you can get back to playing. We’ll do our best to help you resolve this quickly.

reviews.review_description:
This game is a life changer for me. I make sure to do all the light workouts first for a warm up, and then dive into the others. I am 53 and having fun while burning calories and becoming more fit. This was so worth it! 
The trainers are fun, the music is ok, not my favorite, but the rest is so good, I'm glad I downloaded the game.

The dance moves definitely have you break a good sweat and quickly. I love that! However, I every time I play, it glitches. The dance moves freeze or it becomes off centered and I can’t realign or anything. I just turn it off because now I’ve become frustrated. Hopefully this can get fixed soon

developer_response.body: Hello, We’re really sorry the game is behaving like this — that’s not usual and we understand how frustrating it can be. If you contact us at support@odderslab.com, we’ll be happy to help you troubleshoot and resolve the issue so you can play normally again as soon as possible. Thanks for your patience, and we appreciate you letting us know.

reviews.review_description: Great dance routines. It offers more range of motion and gets the hips involved. Very fluid and less physically jarring than body combat. My only criticism 8 pro routines can get old fast
Good workouts, while it fails to launch and remains stuck on the loading screen sometimes.
I cannot get it to load anything. Just the loading screen.

developer_response.body: Hello! We’re sorry for the inconvenience and for the trouble you’re experiencing. Please contact us at support@odderslab.com with your username, and we’ll provide a series of recommendations while we investigate your case to get the game working as soon as possible. Thank you!

reviews.review_title: How do I delete it's taking up space.
reviews.review_description: This app is taking up space in my quest 2, how to delete it completely?

developer_response.body:
Hello. You can fully uninstall the app from your Quest 2, and here are your options:
Press the Meta button to open the universal menu, go to Library and find the app, select the three dots (⋯) next to the app, choose Uninstall and confirm.

Using SideQuest: You can also remove it completely using SideQuest with a custom command. If you’d like, we can explain the exact steps by email (support@odderslab.com)

Also, If you’ve enjoyed the game overall, we’d really appreciate it if you could consider updating your review — it helps us a lot and supports development.

reviews.review_description: This app is great for beginners hip hop or easy dancing. I need something more advanced and more on learning steps.

I’ve been using the game for a short time and don’t yet have a final opinion. There’s definitely progress, good physical effort, sweat, and fatigue — which I like.

However, I really dislike that after every song, you have to return to the main menu and start over. The game is quite heavy and loads very slowly, which breaks the rhythm. Standing in the dark, waiting while the game keeps loading… it really ruins the flow.

It would be great to have a “repeat” or “replay” button after finishing a track, so you could immediately play it again without going back to the main menu. That would make the experience much smoother and save time.

Other users pointed this out over a year ago, but unfortunately, the developers haven’t done anything to improve this.

It would also be great to have an option to set a daily calorie goal and then track your progress. The calorie tracking system still feels unclear to me.
too difficult and a replay button is absolutely needed

I'm a big fan of LesMills  Bodycombat, which I have been using for a long time. The Dance app is different; it's lighter cardio and much more difficult to master. But I like it, especially because it forces me to use the legs more intensively. Given the limitation of the VR set, which only tracks the position of the head and the hands, I think that the trainers and app developers did a great job at guiding the player through the dance moves. I doubt I'll ever be able to actually use the moves on a real dance floor, but playing the app is a fun mild cardio activity. 
As other users pointed out, it's a bit annoying that I can't just hit replay at the end of a dance, so I don't have to wait for the app to load again, but I can live with it. Also, the app is 15GB, so if you're short on storage space, that may be a problem.
Its awesome!
I just got this game last night but I was excited to get a dance in this morning! It’s really fun. And doable.

I purchased this game a couple weeks ago. The first few times I used the app, everything worked perfectly! However every single time after that, the instructor just freezes up and all I can see is the circles moving which I am supposed to follow and I can hear the instructor voice but the character remains frozen. It is impossible for me to learn the steps if I am unable to see the instructor performing the moves?

developer_response.body: Hello! We are very sorry to hear that you are experiencing this issue with the game. Please contact us at support@odderslab.com and provide us with your username and a description of the problem. This will help us investigate and assist you as quickly as possible. We apologize for any inconvenience this may have caused.

reviews.review_description:
but unfortunately it's hard to get into the flow and that's because the coach's voice is louder than the music and I can't get into the flow. I will give 5 stars if you could turn the coach's voice down/louder or turn it off completely.

Overall, its a nice  Workout App with good graphics and you even get some dance skills.

The game looks really fun and I think I might even learn a little bit of dance,  that I don't know at all. But for us who aren't dancers and don't know anything about a dance, you should give us at least 10 minutes that has very few steps and is repeated continuously so that we can learn them little by little. I don't know if there is one, but the ones that have a lot of time I see are also very difficult. It's very tiring to get in and in 3 minutes have to get out again and do the same thing again unless you make a button inside that we can press to continue the same game without having to go to the menu again.

reviews.review_title: Good workout but Confusing Song Selectio
reviews.review_description:
I do like this app and the music is good. For some reason the song selection is very confusing and its hard to know the type of workout or difficulty or why they are ordered as they are... Or whether you have done the workout before recently etc.
Most importantly I see no way to change the instructor voice volume. The instructors are fine but I find their commentary distracting and wish I could mute/turn them down so I can enjoy the music and the movement.
I want to love this but the game is not loading

developer_response.body: Hello! We are very sorry to hear that you are experiencing this issue with the game. Please contact us at support@odderslab.com and provide us with your username and a description of the problem so we can assist you. Thanks!

reviews.review_title: I LOVE it - gets my legs burning
reviews.review_description:
Update part 2: They added more content and there's a new playlist feature - they're doing great things with it. Still love it. 
Update: All the instructors are great. Intro still a bit too flashy/siezure-y. Still 5 stars after playing a lot more of it!!
Self-proclaimed dancephobe and wallflower here. I am too shy to even go to a private dance lesson, but VR dance class is just my speed. I am loving Gandalf and Bianca as my teachers so far. I'd never get teachers this good in my town. This gets my heart rate up and my legs burning (thanks to Bianca's squat moves). So up it goes to the top of my fitness app list. I have two feature requests: 1. When doing a short dance lesson, at the end I get a "menu" button to go back to the main library - I'd love to have a "do it again" option because I often want to repeat the short lessons and improve on them. Going into the menu feels like a few too many clicks to repeat. 2. Reduce the flashy flashy intro scene or let me skip it. It's just too much. Thanks for a nice app Les Mills inc - there is greatness brewing here in the world of VR private dance lessons.

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for that fantastic feedback! We love to hear your suggestions and we are glad you are enjoying the app, keep the good vibes and rhythm flowing!

reviews.review_title: Bought the Les Mills bundle-neither work
reviews.review_description: I purchased the Les Mills package, which includes Body Combat and Les Mills Dance, but neither app is working. Even during the introduction, the speech is choppy, and both apps lag, making them unusable. I deleted and reinstalled them, but the issue persists. I was really looking forward to trying both, but if the developers don’t fix this, I will request a refund. All my other apps work fine, and my Quest 3 is fully updated. Please help me find a solution. Thank you!

developer_response.body:
Hi sassyg0117!
Thanks for reaching out.  We’re very sorry to hear about your experience. To assist you as quickly as possible, could you please send us an email at info@odderslab.com with your Quest username? Thank you for your patience, and sorry again for the inconvenience caused.

reviews.review_title: Pl create "Try Again" button for short
reviews.review_description:
For short 3 ninute sessions pl have a retry button. It's too much to go back to main menu and wait for loading.  

The app is super useful. I use it for workout and fun

I only played one session. I'm a trainer. I work in fitness 40 hours a week. This is so fun and so healthy for the brain, strength and range of motion. ￼I don't even realize I'm working out. I'm having so much fun Thank you. ￼

I purchased the game, but it didn't work.There was a loading animation that I was not able to pass through. I was never able to get a refund. I am very disappointed!

DO NOT BUY THE GAME IF YOU ARE NOT READY TO LOSE YOUR MONEY

developer_response.body:
Hello! We’re very sorry to hear about your experience. To assist you as quickly as possible, could you please send us an email at info@odderslab.com with your headset model and username?

Thank you for your patience, and we sincerely apologize for the inconvenience caused.

reviews.review_description: The overall experience is good. However, as a newer player. There is no way to follow those dancers’ footsteps. There’s no learning section for that. Suggested to add some videos for practice leg movement but not only for body.

developer_response.body: Hello baranshad, thanks for your review and feedback. We will keep adding improvements in future updates thanks to your feedback!

reviews.review_title: good for someone, not for me
reviews.review_description: like the other game, it's alright as an exercise game, but terrible if you want a music/dancing game. the music stinks, the moves are taught poorly, and it's barely in time with the music. but, it does get you moving, so if your main focus is sweating but you don't care about the music/rhythm game aspects, this might be right for you

There’s only like 4 sessions each for four categories (vibe, pop, club and jam; and they average around 10 mins long). 

Then another 8 total for the choreo side (2 for each category). 

That’s it. 

Stars are for the jam and vibe section.

I’ve been getting into this game in the last few weeks. It is much harder than other dance like rhythm games because the targets are not coming toward you so it is harder to precisely hit them.

The menu is driving me insane though. The rows just keep scrolling when you are trying to select or start a dance routine. As soon as your cursor is on a row it just keeps scrolling from right to left or from left to right, it is so jittery. Today it took me several minutes to select and start a certain routine. I don’t understand what’s wrong because I don’t have this problem with les mills bodycombat from the same developer. Please fix this because it is so frustrating. It makes me just want to quit the game instead of start a dance routine.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback, we will make improvements in future updates!

reviews.review_title: Awful experience if you're not a dancer
reviews.review_description:
Love the workout app so I bought this believing they'd produce something great but it isn't. I'm not a dancer and this app does not do anything to help you become one. It's like they wanted to make the cues as inconspicuous as possible so it looks like you're just dancing. That's nice for folks that can dance. For those that want to learn, it's not. There's nothing to tell you what you're doing wrong. You have no idea if you are going the wrong way, in time, following correctly or anything. I didn't get any cue from the short, beginning ones that I tried after the tutorials so I had no clue if I was doing the moves correctly. If cues are there, they are of no value because they are not prominent at all. W

This is one of those situations in which they needed beta testers that were not fanboys of the Body Combat game to give them real, critical feedback on this game during the design process. The instructors are great dancers and I'm sure they are great people but they do not exude any sort of warmth or teaching vibe. It's more about showing us how great they are, not how great we can be. I feel out of place, like I don't belong in the same room with them or at least only to admire them and understand that I'll never be as good. This is the vibe that's put forth. 

The moves themselves are cheesy and a bit cringe. If they were more aerobic and basic, folks could modify them as they like and keep with the theme, while those of us that don't want to look like we're pretending we're in high school musical could perform the more aerobic type moves and get the same benefit. I see the same comment parroted back by the developers about how they are improving the game due to community feedback but I've seen none of it and short of a complete revamp of the game, it was a waste of money for me. We need clear, visual feedback of how we are doing or what's the point?

developer_response.body: Hello cvan65, thanks for your honest feedback. We will take notes to make improvements in the future. We hope you can enjoy Les Mills Dance then!

reviews.review_description: I was really hoping it would be  better after Dance Central was discontinued . It’s so basic and considering  technology has even improved, the content should as well. I’m so disappointed I didn’t request a refund.  I do not recommend if you have ever take ANY fitness dance class.  This reminds me of Zumba Gold - even the “advanced” dance options that are available.

reviews.review_title: Such a fun dance work out!
reviews.review_description:
I love Les Mills Body Combat, so I knew that I would really enjoy this dance game!  It is so fun and such a great work out!  Learning is quick and even once you get to the choreo lessons/sessions are short (less than 20 minutes) so it’s not too daunting.

One thing I noticed was the Pop Dive In Choreo seems to have the lessons backwards.  Other than that, everything has been great!

I love body combat and this had so much potential. It’s just not a fun game and it’s not much of a workout either. If it was fun I’d play it for fun. If it was a hard workout I’d do it for the workout. Unfortunately this is neither.

developer_response.body:
Hello PipSquad, we are sorry to hear Les Mills XR Dance isn't your cup of tea.

We have been making improvements based on community feedback. Our latest update has better visual feedback and now dancing is more fun and intuitive. Please try Les Mills XR Dance again and let us know if your experience is better now.

reviews.review_title: Variety of Songs Would Be Great
reviews.review_description:
Would it be possible to add a little more variety to the songs? Most of the ones in the app do not want to make me dance/are similar in style/lack playfulness/musicality. 
Other than that, I love both of the Les Mills apps, one of my favourite app ever is the bodycombat one.

As someone with zero dancing experience, I enjoyed it a lot. I appreciate that the dances include the use of the legs, often forgotten in other rythm games. I specially like Gandalf, he explains everything in great detail and makes the routines fun.

I've been using the Supernatural app and then bought the Les Mills Combat to see what everyone was talking about. When I saw Les Mills dance, I thought it would be fun to learn new movements that I've seen in music videos and K-Pop. I was excited to try a dance app since I've missed choreography. The choreography movements are good, and while they can progress quickly, they repeat the patterns enough to get it.  However, the scoring feedback requires movements in ways that didn't feel coordinated, so I did my own thing and still got "no misses." A false positive since I didn't get all the haptic feedback. So far, the music is not recognizable like Supernatural, I've heard techno and Spanish. I'm on the fence about keeping this app to hope that it will get better. Between Supernatural and Combat, I believe I will get a good workout.

developer_response.body:
Hello Nikki, we are sorry to hear your experience with Les Mills XR Dance wasn't what you were expecting. 

We have been making improvements based on community feedback. Our latest update has better visual feedback and now dancing is more fun and intuitive. Please try Les Mills XR Dance again and let us know if your experience is better now

reviews.review_title: Promising but major flaws at launch
reviews.review_description:
This app could be GREAT, but in its launch state there are Three major flaws that make it VERY unenjoyable!  1) The hand targets are too close to the plane of your body and you CANNOT see them clearly to guide your hand/arm movements!  2) The "center" calibration puts the center of your in-game dance space too far behind you!  3) You cannot swap colors so pink is on the right and blue is on the left! Nor can you customize the hand colors. At the very least, we should have a config option to SWAP the colors so they match the default hand colors for Synth Riders. You realize those of us who would play a dance game like this also probably love Synth Riders, right? They put pink on the right and blue on the left, by default. Those of us who've spent a year or more in Synth Riders and stuck with the default hand colors have muscle memory about which color is which hand that is difficult to undo.

Fix these three problems and you'll have a solid 5 from me. Until then, the game is NOT fun and difficult to follow. Suggestion for fixing the target visibility: just extend a somewhat transparent "cylinder silhoutte" out about a foot or two towards the trainer. This way the target decal stays where it is for hand placement, but we can still see the extruded silhouette in our peripheral vision as we're looking strait at the instructor's face.

developer_response.body: Hello yokaidesu, thanks for your honest feedback. We have been making improvements based on your comments. Our latest update has better visual feedback and now dancing is more fun and intuitive. Please try Les Mills XR Dance again and let us know if your experience is better now!

reviews.review_title: As a professional dancer this is amazing
reviews.review_description: I don’t ever leave reviews on anything, I had to with this amazing experience! definitely worth it as a 10+ years professional dancer, amazing work out and good training to warm up. Such an unreal and incredible app
I’m not crazy about this app. I expected more….

developer_response.body: Hello Rosann, we are sorry to hear Les Mills XR Dance, didn't match your expectations. We will keep making improvements so please come back in future updates to see if it's your cup of tea or not.

reviews.review_title: Fantastic and fun! VR at it’s very BEST
reviews.review_description: I do a variety of workout apps every morning with a focus of getting my heart rate up. The one exception is Les Mills Dance XR which does a decent job getting my heart rate up, but does an extraordinary job teaching dance and rhythm in the most compelling and entertaining way imaginable. Instructors are super outstanding and fun, and every session is a blast. It’s one of my very few ‘everyday’ apps and the one I most look forward to. Thank you!

reviews.review_title: Less Mills Dance Delivers Fitness & Fun
reviews.review_description: Less Mills Dance takes virtual reality dancing to a whole new level, offering an incredible dance experience that seamlessly combines fitness and fun. As a fan of Body Combat, I was initially skeptical about dancing in VR, but once I immersed myself in the game, I was hooked. The precision in following the coach's movements, coupled with responsive controller feedback, creates an immersive dance atmosphere that feels nothing short of magical. It's like the fitness version of Just Dance, making it accessible even for those who aren't typically drawn to dancing. The game's diverse choreographies and approachable difficulty levels make it perfect for beginners, boosting confidence and refining dance skills. With a variety of music styles and visually stunning environments, Less Mills Dance ensures you'll not only break a sweat but also enjoy every moment of your dance workout. Whether you're a seasoned dancer or a VR exercise enthusiast, this game is a must-try, turning skeptics into dancing enthusiasts with its fun and sweaty appeal.
